Helping the World Feel Better — One Breath at a Time

Empowering veterans, active-duty military, first responders, and their families through trauma-conscious yoga — completely free of charge.

Our Programs Donate Now
Who We Are

Our Mission

Connected Warriors is dedicated to improving the physical and mental well-being of veterans, active-duty military, first responders, and their families through trauma-conscious yoga and mindfulness programs. We believe that healing begins with connection — to breath, to body, and to community. Our evidence-based programs are offered completely free, ensuring that every warrior who needs support can access it without barriers.

LCDR Kenneth A. Bingham, USN (Ret.)

Leadership

Commander Kenneth A. Bingham

Kenneth A. Bingham is a retired United States Navy Lieutenant Commander, educator, mentor, and yoga instructor dedicated to serving veterans, first responders, service members, and their families. His distinguished 22-year Navy career as a fighter pilot included flying the F-4 Phantom and F-14 Tomcat, with more than 650 carrier landings — including service during Desert Storm and deployments worldwide.

Following his retirement from the Navy, Ken became a Senior Naval Science Instructor at Boca Raton Community High School, leading the NJROTC program to back-to-back National Championships in 2011 and 2014. Introduced to yoga in 2010 through Connected Warriors founder Judy Weaver, Ken witnessed its transformative impact on cadets and veterans. He later joined Connected Warriors' Board of Directors and earned his Registered Yoga Teacher (CWRYT 200) certification in 2016. He continues to teach trauma-conscious yoga to veterans, active-duty military, first responders, and their families.

Our Community

Who We Serve

Veterans

Honoring those who have served with healing programs designed for their unique experiences.

Active-Duty

Supporting service members currently serving with tools for resilience and well-being.

First Responders

Providing relief to those who protect our communities and face daily trauma.

Family Members

Extending care to family members who carry the weight of service alongside their loved ones.

Proud Partner of the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs

Connected Warriors partners with the VA to deliver trauma-conscious yoga programming at VA Medical Centers and Vet Centers across the nation, reaching warriors where they already receive care.

What We Do

Our Programs

Trauma-Conscious Yoga

Evidence-based yoga classes specifically designed for trauma survivors, offered at VA Medical Centers, Vet Centers, and community locations.

  • Clinically informed curriculum
  • Safe, invitational language
  • Accessible to all levels
  • Always 100% free

Teacher Training

Comprehensive certification programs for yoga teachers who want to serve the military and first responder communities with trauma-informed techniques.

  • Yoga Alliance certified
  • Military cultural competency
  • Trauma-informed methodology
  • Ongoing mentorship support
"Connected Warriors gave me the tools to find peace again."

— A Connected Warrior

In Honor of Beau

Our story began in 2007 when Army Ranger Beau MacVane, with five tours in the Middle East, came home to South Florida and was diagnosed with Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS). His physician at the Miami VA Medical Center recommended yoga and Beau found Judy Weaver at Yoga South in Boca Raton. Judy watched in awe as Beau and his family incorporated the teachings of yoga to both battle and ultimately make peace with his body’s inevitable decline.

When Beau passed away surrounded by friends and family, he left this world with an indomitable inner strength and peace-of-mind more powerful than any mere physical malady. Beau’s determination and warrior spirit inspired Judy to teach free classes with her student, Ralph Iovino, who brought veterans and their families.

Soon people were asking for more classes and volunteering to help—the beginning of Connected Warriors.

“The Connected Warriors program has taken me from hobbling around with an injured knee and lower back, to almost 100% rehabilitated in less than two years. CW has not only helped mend me physically, but mentally as well. Anytime, anywhere, deployed or at home—I make my own yoga studio whenever I please.”

— 1SG, US ARMY ACTIVE DUTY
